commands.author: Ittiz

target.title: feat(plugins): add Control UI entry points
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but there was no generic way for a plugin to register a first-class Control UI navigation entry.
- What changed: Added generic Plugin UI Entry Points across the plugin registry, gateway protocol, launch-token plumbing, and Control UI navigation/rendering.
- Security follow-up: Plugin UI entry registration, launch/session auth, and iframe bridge proxying now validate canonical decoded plugin roots, so encoded dot-segment paths cannot let one plugin UI session authorize sibling plugin routes.
- Scope boundary: This PR does not add the Session Search plugin or any plugin-specific UI. The proof plugin lives separately on [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).

## What Problem This Solves

Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but before this PR they had no generic, first-class way to place a Control UI navigation entry that launches a plugin-owned UI through the gateway authorization boundary. This PR adds that contract while keeping plugin entry paths and iframe bridge requests confined to the owning plugin root.

## Working Example Plugin

The working example is the Session Search plugin on the plugin-only proof branch:

- Branch: [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin)
- README: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/README.md)
- Entry point registration: [`index.ts`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/index.ts)
- Manifest: [`openclaw.plugin.json`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/openclaw.plugin.json)

That branch intentionally contains only plugin files, not the OpenClaw source tree.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof

- Current head tested: `23fe820d30319b9ec40a5470c2e562d78c301373`.
- Behavior addressed: Plugin UI entry points can be listed/launched/rendered; decoded/canonicalized plugin paths remain confined to the owning plugin root across registration, launch/session auth, and sandbox bridge/proxy requests.
- Real environment tested: rebuilt exact-head local Linux checkout, throwaway loopback gateway on `127.0.0.1:19888`, temporary plugin loaded through `plugins.load.paths`, Control UI driven in a real headless browser, plus direct HTTP route checks.

Commands run after the current fix:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts ui/src/ui/open-external-url.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/schema-normalization-runtime-contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs ciArtifacts
node .artifacts/pr80388-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live browser/plugin-route evidence from the throwaway exact-head gateway:

```json
{
  "head": "OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (23fe820)",
  "controlUiUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:19888/overview#token=REDACTED",
  "pluginEntryVisible": true,
  "iframeMarker": "PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_IFRAME_READY",
  "bridgeText": "BRIDGE_STATUS: 200 {\"ok\":true,\"marker\":\"PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_API_READY\",\"pluginId\":\"proof-entry\"}",
  "directUnauthStatus": 401,
  "directAuthStatus": 200,
  "directAuthBody": "{\"ok\":true,\"marker\":\"PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_API_READY\",\"pluginId\":\"proof-entry\"}"
}
```

Focused test evidence:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 91.89s

$ node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
package-exported forbidden subpaths: 0

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.84s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts` rejects Plugin UI Entry Point sessions when decoded path candidates leave the plugin root.
- `src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` rejects encoded dot-segment sibling escapes at entry-point registration.
- `ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts` rejects external, contextual, cross-plugin, and encoded sibling paths.
- `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts` verifies sandboxed in-app plugin requests are proxied only for plugin-owned paths and that stale iframe bridge ports cannot service current-frame requests.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes. Plugins can expose Control UI entry points.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. Entry-point launch uses short-lived Plugin UI launch/session tokens.
- Data access scope changed? Yes. Entry points declare required operator scopes.
- Mitigation: Entry-point paths are constrained to canonical plugin-owned roots; launch/session auth and iframe proxying reject decoded sibling escapes; direct unauthenticated plugin route access remains blocked.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: feat(agent-sdk): add packaging spec + skeleton
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the Agent SDK Packaging spec (v0.3 draft) and implementation skeleton, with hardened package path validation, schema-backed package config, quarantine read-only tool restrictions, shared network policy classification, CLI build wiring, and deterministic behavior proof output.

## What's Included

- `docs/specs/agent-sdk-packaging.md` — Full spec: manifest schema, integrity verification, secret scoping, network egress, mutable instruction policy, declarative upgrades, behavior harness, and config compiler requirements.
- `packages/agent-sdk/` — TypeScript types, CLI commands, policy helpers, config compiler, test harness scaffold, examples, fixtures, and tests.
- Agent package config schema support for the `agentPackages` compiler output block.

## Key Design Decisions

- **openclaw.integrity.json** (not lockfile) — content integrity, not dependency resolution.
- **denyMutableInstructionFiles: true** — instruction files are prompt-layer code.
- **Config compiler dry-run** — config output is now backed by the live config schema.
- **No scripts, no hooks** — purely declarative upgrades.
- **Fail-closed file handling** — package sources and workspace destinations are confined before copy, remove, pack, and validate operations.

## Status

Draft PR. Current branch includes the spec, implementation skeleton, hardened safety checks, targeted tests, and real checkout proof below.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent SDK package workflow builds CLI entrypoints, validates package-owned files before copy/remove/hash operations, keeps quarantine tool access read-only, uses shared network special-use IP classification, validates emitted `agentPackages` config through schema support. The CLI `test` command now runs real validation, integrity, network, secret, tool, quarantine, compiler, and upgrade checks — no unconditional PASS output. Package policy fields (maxTokensPerTurn, allowedModels, tools, sandbox) are scoped under `agentPackages.packages.<name>.*` by default instead of leaking into `agents.defaults.*`. Non-canonical `source: "gateway"` SecretRef mappings are skipped with a compiler warning.

- **Real environment tested**: Real local checkout at `/tmp/openclaw-agent-sdk-pr-worktree`, branch `feat/agent-sdk-packaging`, commit `f110b84284d3`, Node 22 / pnpm / tsdown 0.22 from the OpenClaw monorepo toolchain.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
# 1. Build the CLI
npx tsdown packages/agent-sdk/src/index.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/cli.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/hash.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/pack.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/validate.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/enable.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/disable.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/secrets.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/network.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/compiler.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/live.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/upgrade.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/quarantine/mutation.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/test.ts --no-config --platform node --format esm --dts --out-dir packages/agent-sdk/dist --clean

# 2. Run the CLI test command against the valid-pack fixture
node packages/agent-sdk/dist/cli.mjs test packages/agent-sdk/src/__fixtures__/valid-pack

# 3. Run the full agent-sdk test suite (8 files, 164 tests)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-sdk/src/pack.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/policy.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/compiler.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/live.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/upgrade.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/quarantine/mutation.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/enable.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/test.harness.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**: CLI test command output (real checks):

```
$ node packages/agent-sdk/dist/cli.mjs test packages/agent-sdk/src/__fixtures__/valid-pack
Agent SDK behavior proof summary: PASS
Required proofs: 21
PASS agent.manifest.valid
PASS agent.integrity.valid
PASS agent.integrity.mismatchFailsClosed
PASS agent.installedState.valid
PASS agent.installedState.driftQuarantines
PASS agent.instructionFile.driftQuarantines
PASS agent.mutableInstructionFile.deniedByPolicy
PASS agent.requiredTool.missingFailsClosed
PASS agent.requiredPlugin.missingFailsClosed
PASS agent.requiredSecret.missingFailsClosed
PASS agent.secretScope.enforced
PASS agent.deniedTool.blocked
PASS agent.externalContentToExec.blocked
PASS agent.outbound.requiresApproval
PASS agent.workspaceEscape.blocked
PASS agent.schedule.disabledByDefault
PASS agent.privateNetwork.blocked
PASS agent.dnsRebinding.blocked
PASS agent.sandbox.required
PASS agent.configCompiler.dryRunValidates
PASS agent.upgrade.permissionExpansionRequiresApproval
```

Unit tests (8 files, 164 tests):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-sdk/src/pack.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/policy.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/compiler.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/live.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/upgrade.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/quarantine/mutation.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/enable.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/test.harness.test.ts
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The CLI `test` command runs real validation (schema, integrity, mutable instruction policy), hash verification, network policy checks, secret scope enforcement, tool allow/deny policy, quarantine/mutation detection, compiler dry-run validation, and upgrade permission checks. Each proof ID is only marked PASS if the actual check passes. Package policy is scoped under `agentPackages.packages.<name>.*` by default, preventing silent global-default mutation. Non-canonical `gateway` SecretRefs are skipped with compiler warnings. All 164 unit tests pass across 8 test files.

- **What was not tested**: No live gateway mutation, live schedule routing, live channel delivery, or real secret provider access was exercised. The `test` command validates the package fixture locally but does not install or enable the package in a live OpenClaw instance. Upgrade behavior is tested via unit tests only, not against a real running gateway.

commands.author: wangwllu

target.title: fix(announce-delivery): backfill effectiveDirectOrigin.to from requester session entry
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a silent media-delivery drop when `image_generate` (or any
generated-media task) is invoked off the direct-reply path — heartbeat, cron,
subagent spawn, etc. — where `agentTo` is undefined.

In that case the persisted `requesterOrigin` carries `channel` and
`accountId` but no `to`, and every downstream `Boolean(channel && to)`
deliverability gate short-circuits. The artifact is generated and written to
disk, but never delivered; `task_runs.status` is then marked failed with
`completion delivery failed after successful generation`. User sees nothing.

The fix is a one-merge change in
`src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`: move the already-loaded
`requesterEntry` slightly earlier, derive a delivery-context fallback from it
via the existing `deliveryContextFromSession`, and merge that into
`completionExternalFallbackOrigin` *before* `effectiveDirectOrigin` is
computed.

The in-source comment at that exact site (previously at lines 1202–1205)
already named this gap; the patch makes the comment accurate by acting on it.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#86034 (Hypothesis A only — origin missing `to`).

## What the patch does

- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` — Move the `loadRequesterSessionEntry` call up; compute `requesterSessionDeliveryFallback = deliveryContextFromSession(requesterEntry)`; merge it into the existing fallback chain.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — New regression: completion origin missing `to`, requester session entry has `lastTo` → `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is populated and external delivery is selected (`deliver: true`).

Total non-test diff: ~14 lines.

## Risk / blast radius

Low. The merge happens inside an `expectsCompletionMessage` branch that
already documents this exact intent. `mergeDeliveryContext` refuses to cross
channels, so we cannot synthesize a delivery target that wasn't already in the
requester session entry. Happy-path tests
(`subagent-registry-lifecycle`, `image-generate-tool`,
`image-generation-task-status`) confirm no regression when the origin already
carries `to`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: When `image_generate` is invoked off the direct-reply path (heartbeat / cron / subagent spawn) on a Telegram surface, the generated artifact lands on disk but is never delivered to the chat; the user sees nothing while `task_runs.status` is marked failed with `completion delivery failed after successful generation`. After this patch, on the same Telegram path, the generated image is delivered to the chat.

Real environment tested: Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end via `openclaw-mantis` workflow, executed against a real OpenClaw setup driving a real Telegram client. Two runs were captured side-by-side:
- Baseline: `main` at `6d5061c2…` (pre-patch tip), real Telegram Desktop transport.
- Candidate: this PR head at `974b07b0…` (the original PR head Mantis observed before today's force-push rebase to `f6a45b1f`; the patch hunk is identical, so the observed transport-level behavior carries over). Real Telegram Desktop transport.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end proof was driven by openclaw-mantis.
# The Mantis workflow run that captured this PR's real-behavior evidence:
gh run view 26902454818 --repo openclaw/openclaw
gh api repos/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts

# Local supplemental regression suite on the patched head (f6a45b1f):
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
- Mantis live Telegram Desktop run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818
- Mantis artifacts (recordings / screenshots / MP4s side-by-side): https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts/7393384873
- Mantis comment with embedded GIFs / screenshots / MP4s is in this PR thread.

```text
Mantis run 26902454818 verdicts (live Telegram Desktop transport):
- Scenario:  telegram-desktop-proof
  Baseline (main 6d5061c2…):       pass — expected baseline visual proof captured
  Candidate (PR head 974b07b0…):   pass — expected candidate visual proof captured
  Overall:                         true
```

Observed result after fix: On the previously broken off-direct-reply path the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop client visibly received the generated image. Local supplemental regression: `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` (with the new regression case), `media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`, `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts`, `image-generate-tool.test.ts`, `image-generation-task-status.test.ts` — all 5 files, 194 tests, green on the patched head. The new regression case in `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` fails without the patch and passes with it; it asserts that when the completion origin is missing `to` and `requesterEntry.lastTo` is populated, `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is backfilled and `deliver: true` is selected — exactly the gate that was short-circuiting in the reported bug.

What was not tested: Crabbox / Testbox suites and `tsgo` typecheck were skipped per repo guidance for this checkout. No live Telegram Bot API account other than the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop scenario was exercised. Hypothesis B (the `wakeTaskCompletion` retry asymmetry described under Follow-up) is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

## Follow-up (Hypothesis B)

Hypothesis B from the review — `wakeTaskCompletion → false` does not retry
`tryDeliverMediaGenerationDirect` (only the `throw` branch does), and the
in-wake fallback gates on `MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` which
excludes `requester_abandoned` and the `deliver=false` reason from Hypothesis
A — is a real but separable robustness gap. It crosses the larger
`MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` contract surface and warrants a
"delivery contract decision" maintainer review on its own. See
`src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts:425-468` for the
asymmetric branch.

Fixing A alone unblocks the reported Telegram repro: once `origin.to` is
populated, the in-wake direct fallback (which also checks `origin.to`)
likewise becomes viable.

target.title: Avoid copying process.env in ingress queue state DB opens
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94571.

This changes the channel ingress queue state DB opener so a queue-specific `stateDir` no longer copies the full `process.env` on every queue operation.

Before:

```ts
{ ...process.env, OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: stateDir }
```

After:

```ts
Object.assign(Object.create(process.env), { OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: stateDir })
```

The overlay keeps inherited lookups such as `HOME` / `OPENCLAW_HOME`, while only `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` is an own enumerable key.

## Why

Telegram isolated polling ingress drains its durable local spool every ~500ms. Each drain calls channel ingress queue operations such as `recoverStaleClaims`, `listClaims`, and `listPending`. Those operations open the OpenClaw state DB through `src/channels/message/ingress-queue.ts`.

In Kubernetes pods with service links enabled, `process.env` can contain thousands of service variables. Repeatedly spreading `process.env` in this hot path can dominate the drain loop and saturate CPU/event loop responsiveness.

Observed on OpenClaw 2026.6.6 in Kubernetes:

- `Object.keys(process.env).length`: 9274
- `{ ...process.env }` once: 917ms to 1192ms
- queue wrapper `listPending()` once: ~982ms
- queue wrapper `listClaims()` once: ~816ms
- queue wrapper `recoverStaleClaims()` once: ~869ms

Control checks showed SQLite itself was not the bottleneck:

- direct `SELECT count(*)` x10000: ~118ms
- direct OpenClaw state DB + Kysely query: ~0-2ms

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram isolated polling ingress repeatedly opens the channel ingress queue state DB with a queue-specific `stateDir`. The old path copied every `process.env` key on each queue operation, which becomes a runtime hot path in Kubernetes pods with thousands of service env vars.
- **Real environment tested**: Real Kubernetes OpenClaw runtime pod running OpenClaw 2026.6.6 with Telegram isolated ingress enabled and Kubernetes service env links present. The pod had 9274 environment keys.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Applied the same prototype env-overlay change used by this PR to the running runtime experiment, then ran node-based queue diagnostics inside the pod against the OpenClaw state DB and Telegram ingress queue operations.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live output from the real runtime experiment and the follow-up node behavior check:

```text
before fix: env keys = 9274
before fix: { ...process.env } once = 917ms to 1192ms
before fix: ingress queue listPending() once = ~982ms
before fix: ingress queue listClaims() once = ~816ms
before fix: ingress queue recoverStaleClaims() once = ~869ms
control: direct sqlite SELECT count(*) x10000 = ~118ms
control: direct OpenClaw state DB + Kysely query = ~0-2ms
after fix: prototype env overlay own keys = 1 (OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR)
after fix: inherited HOME lookup preserved through the overlay
after fix: queue wrapper calls returned to normal SQLite-level timings after warmup instead of spending ~0.8-1.2s copying env per queue operation
local exact-helper check: {"spreadOwnKeys":10002,"overlayOwnKeys":1,"overlayHome":"/home/openclaw","spreadMs":3,"overlayMs":0}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The expensive work moved from enumerating thousands of env keys per DB open to a one-key prototype overlay. In the real runtime experiment this removed the observed ~0.8-1.2s queue-operation delay caused by env copying while preserving inherited env lookups needed by state DB resolution.
- **What was not tested**: Full local workspace Vitest was not completed in the sparse checkout because it triggered dependency installation for 155 workspace projects and the registry install was interrupted. The focused regression test is included for CI on the full repository checkout.

## Reproduction

A lightweight reproduction is to run ingress queue operations with a large env and an explicit queue `stateDir`:

```js
for (let i = 0; i < 10000; i++) {
  process.env[`KUBERNETES_SERVICE_${i}`] = "tcp://10.0.0.1:443";
}
```

Then call the queue with a custom state dir:

```ts
const queue = createChannelIngressQueue({
  channelId: "telegram",
  accountId: "default",
  stateDir,
});

await queue.recoverStaleClaims({ staleMs: 0 });
await queue.listClaims();
await queue.listPending({ limit: 1000, orderBy: "id" });
```

The old implementation enumerates/copies every env key before each DB open; the new implementation does not.

## Tests

- Added a focused regression test that constructs a 10k-key env object and verifies the state-dir env overlay has only `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` as its own key while preserving inherited `HOME` lookup.
- Ran `git diff --check` locally.
- Ran a small Node behavior check showing the overlay has one own key and still resolves inherited `HOME`.

I did not complete the local Vitest run because this sparse checkout triggered full workspace dependency installation for 155 workspace projects and the registry install was interrupted. CI should run the normal project test suite on the full repository checkout.

commands.author: ruomuxydt

target.title: fix(config): coerce boolean retry.jitter to number (#52130)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #52130.

`retry.jitter: true` (or `false`) failed Zod validation with "expected number, received boolean", crashing gateway startup on every restart.

## Root cause

`RetryConfigSchema.jitter` is a numeric-only field shared across provider retry configs. A stray boolean value (e.g. copied from older docs or a different tool's config) failed validation before the gateway could start.

## Fix

Per ClawSweeper rank-up feedback and repo policy (malformed/legacy config repair belongs in doctor/migration, not the runtime zod schema), the **runtime schema stays numeric-only** and boolean coercion moves into the **doctor legacy-config migration layer**:

- New migration `legacy-config-migrations.runtime.retry.ts` recursively walks the config tree and coerces any boolean `retry.jitter` (`true` -> `0.1`, `false` -> `0`) across all provider retry configs.
- Registered in `LEGACY_CONFIG_MIGRATIONS_RUNTIME` so `openclaw doctor --fix` applies it.
- A `LegacyConfigRule` lets doctor detect and preview the legacy boolean before `--fix`.
- The runtime `RetryConfigSchema.jitter` remains `z.number().min(0).max(1)` (numeric-only).

Out of scope: changing the runtime schema to accept booleans (intentionally avoided per repo policy).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** A boolean `retry.jitter` value should be repaired by `openclaw doctor --fix` (coerced to a number) instead of crashing gateway startup at validation time.

**Real environment tested:** Windows 10, Node 22.22.3, OpenClaw source checkout at `63c49880`, real Node process driving the real `applyLegacyDoctorMigrations` function.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.runtime.retry.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix (head `63c49880`):**
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
```

Test coverage:
- `retry.jitter: true` -> `0.1` (provider retry path)
- `retry.jitter: false` -> `0`
- numeric `retry.jitter: 0.25` left unchanged
- multiple nested provider retry configs (models.providers + channels) all coerced

**Observed result after fix:** Before, boolean `retry.jitter` crashed validation. After, `openclaw doctor --fix` coerces it to the numeric equivalent and the runtime schema stays numeric-only.

**What was not tested:** Not run against a live gateway restart with a boolean jitter config; the proof drives the real migration function with synthetic provider configs that match the issue's shape.

This PR is AI-assisted; the code, tests, and proof output above were produced and verified in my local environment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

target.title: fix(control-ui): recognize namespaced reasoning tags in thinking extraction
target.body:
## Problem
`extractThinking()` in the Control UI only recognized bare legacy text-block tags such as `<think>` / `<thinking>`. Other parts of the UI already strip/suppress the broader reasoning-tag family, including namespaced MiniMax-style tags such as `<mm:thinking>`.

When a model emits a namespaced reasoning block, the Control UI fallback path can leave the reasoning text in the visible answer instead of extracting it into the thinking section. In practice, because the shared visible-text path (`stripReasoningTagsFromText` in `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`) already strips namespaced tags from the answer, the reasoning block was lost from both views: removed from the visible answer but never surfaced in the thinking panel.

## Fix
Extend the legacy text-block extraction pattern to recognize namespaced reasoning tags (`antml:`, `mm:`) plus `<thought>` / `<antthinking>`, mirroring the exact tag set already recognized by `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`, while preserving the existing bare-tag behavior.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: a message containing a namespaced reasoning tag (`<mm:think>`, `<thought>`, `<antthinking>`, ...) is now surfaced in the thinking panel by `extractThinking()` and kept out of the visible answer by `extractText()`, instead of being lost (stripped from the visible answer but missing from the thinking section).

**Real environment tested**: openclaw repo on this PR branch (`fix/control-ui-mm-thinking-tags`, head `89ce4d57a9`), Node 22 + tsx, importing the real production functions `extractThinking` / `extractText` from the changed file `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts`. No mocks — the proof drives the real extraction path and the real shared visible-text strip path.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.test.ts --run` -> 1 file / 11 tests passed
- A standalone tsx real-runtime script that imports `extractThinking` / `extractText` from `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts` and feeds assistant messages whose text contains namespaced reasoning blocks (`<mm:think>...`, `<thinking>...`, `<thought>...`, `<antthinking>...`, and bare `<think>...` as a regression guard): `node_modules/.bin/tsx proof.mts`
- Negative control: revert only `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts` to its pre-fix parent (`git checkout HEAD~1 -- ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts`) and re-run the same script, then restore (`git checkout HEAD -- ...`).

**Evidence after fix** (patched code, `tsx proof.mts`):
```
[PASS] MiniMax mm: namespaced think tag
   input          : "<mm:think>Weigh option A vs B, pick A.</mm:think>The answer is A."
   extractThinking: "Weigh option A vs B, pick A."  (expected "Weigh option A vs B, pick A.")
   extractText    : "The answer is A."  (visible answer, no reasoning leak)
[PASS] antml: namespaced thinking tag      -> extractThinking: "Consider edge cases first."
[PASS] <thought> tag                       -> extractThinking: "Reasoning trace here."
[PASS] <antthinking> tag                   -> extractThinking: "Hidden chain of thought."
[PASS] plain <think> still works           -> extractThinking: "Step 1."
=== summary ===
ALL 5 cases PASS: namespaced reasoning blocks are surfaced in the thinking panel
and kept out of the visible answer. No reasoning content is lost.
```

**Observed result after fix**: every namespaced reasoning block is surfaced in the thinking panel (`extractThinking` returns the reasoning text) and absent from the visible answer (`extractText` returns only the answer), and the bare-tag case still works.

Negative control on pre-fix code (same script, `extractThinking` regex reverted to the old `/<\s*think(?:ing)?\s*>.../` form) reproduces the bug:
```
[FAIL] MiniMax mm: namespaced think tag    -> extractThinking: null (reasoning lost; visible="The answer is A.")
[PASS] antml: namespaced thinking tag      -> extractThinking: "Consider edge cases first." (bare <thinking> already worked)
[FAIL] <thought> tag                       -> extractThinking: null (reasoning lost)
[FAIL] <antthinking> tag                   -> extractThinking: null (reasoning lost)
[PASS] plain <think> still works           -> extractThinking: "Step 1."
=== summary ===
3/5 cases FAILED: namespaced reasoning block was NOT extracted
=> reasoning content is lost (stripped from visible answer but missing from thinking panel).
```

**What was not tested**: live browser rendering of the Control UI thinking panel and end-to-end streaming from a real MiniMax/namespaced-tag model were not exercised; the proof targets the parser boundary (the changed `extractThinking()` and the shared `extractText()` strip path) directly via real function calls.

## Scope
Small Control UI parser-only change. No runtime API, storage, or protocol changes.

commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds structured external-content provenance to `before_tool_call` hook events and contexts. Policy plugins can now see whether untrusted external content (web_fetch, email, browser, etc.) was present in the model context that led to a tool call.

## What changed

- **New detector** in `src/agents/tool-hook-external-content.ts`: parses production `<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="...">>>` markers from `wrapExternalContent` output, not synthetic `BEGIN` markers.
- **New types** in `src/plugins/hook-types.ts`: `PluginHookExternalContentSource` and `PluginHookExternalContentProvenance`.
- **Hook propagation** in `src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts`: `externalContent` flows through `HookContext`, before_tool_call plugin events, and trusted tool policy events. Provenance is refreshed after each tool result so later hooks in the same run see accumulated sources.
- **Shared hook context** in `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`: `createOpenClawCodingTools` accepts an optional `beforeToolCallHookContext` so callers can share provenance state across wrapped tool instances. The passed-in context is spread as the base, so wrapped core tools inherit `externalContent` from the prompt.
- **Prompt provenance** in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`: initial provenance is detected from the prompt and seeded into the hook context before any tool calls fire.
- **Docs** in `docs/plugins/hooks.md`: documented the new `externalContent` field and source semantics.
- **Bug fix**: `structuredClone` in `recordAdjustedParamsForToolCall` now wrapped in try/catch so hostile proxy objects do not break tool execution.
- **Anti-spoofing fix**: `Source:` labels are only read from the metadata section between the start marker and `---` separator, not from the untrusted content body.

## Why

External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text by `wrapExternalContent`. Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary had no structured signal showing whether external content was present. Policy plugins could see tool name, params, run ID, and derived paths, but not whether the call followed web_fetch, email, browser, or other untrusted external context.

## Production detector

The detector recognizes the production `wrapExternalContent` marker format:

```text
<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="abc123">>>
Source: Web Fetch
---
sanitized content
<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="abc123">>>
```

It scans any string value, including tool results, prompt text, and hook context fields, for these markers and extracts the `Source:` label from the metadata section only. This is the same format that web_fetch, email, and other external-content tools actually produce.

## Wiring fix (ClawSweeper P1)

Previous versions seeded prompt provenance into `catalogToolHookContext` only, but `createOpenClawCodingTools` built its own separate hook context for wrapped core tools. The first real tool call, such as exec or read, after an external-content prompt could reach policies without `externalContent`.

Fix: `createOpenClawCodingTools` now accepts `beforeToolCallHookContext` which is spread as the base of the internal `hookContext`. `attempt.ts` detects prompt provenance before tools are constructed and passes it through.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: External content provenance must survive a same-run OpenClaw runtime path where a web_fetch-style tool result adds production-wrapped untrusted content and a later `exec` tool call reaches a trusted policy hook with structured `externalContent` provenance.
- Real environment tested: PR head `a3df2df2f043706d0a6cf881689d8a348809e676`, local Linux worktree `/home/rev/projects/openclaw-prs/tool-call-external-provenance`, Node.js v24.15.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout, ran a `node --import tsx` live runtime command that imported production OpenClaw modules from `src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime.ts`, `src/plugins/hook-runner-global.ts`, and `src/security/external-content.ts`; registered an active trusted tool policy; wrapped `web_fetch` and `bash` tools with `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook`; executed `web_fetch` first with production `wrapExternalContent(...)` blocks for `web_fetch` and `email`; then executed `bash` as the follow-up tool call.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live stdout from the PR-head command:

```json
{
  "proof": "pr-91800 externalContent propagation live runtime capture",
  "node": "v24.15.0",
  "cwd": "/home/rev/projects/openclaw-prs/tool-call-external-provenance",
  "sharedContextExternalContentAfterFetch": {
    "present": true,
    "sources": [
      "email",
      "web_fetch"
    ]
  },
  "trustedPolicyCalls": [
    {
      "event": {
        "toolName": "web_fetch",
        "runId": "run-fetch-then-exec-live-proof",
        "toolCallId": "fetch-1"
      },
      "ctx": {
        "toolName": "web_fetch",
        "runId": "run-fetch-then-exec-live-proof",
        "toolCallId": "fetch-1"
      }
    },
    {
      "event": {
        "toolName": "exec",
        "runId": "run-fetch-then-exec-live-proof",
        "toolCallId": "exec-1",
        "externalContent": {
          "present": true,
          "sources": [
            "email",
            "web_fetch"
          ]
        }
      },
      "ctx": {
        "toolName": "exec",
        "runId": "run-fetch-then-exec-live-proof",
        "toolCallId": "exec-1",
        "externalContent": {
          "present": true,
          "sources": [
            "email",
            "web_fetch"
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The first `web_fetch` policy event/context had no `externalContent`, then the shared hook context refreshed from the returned production external-content markers to `{"present":true,"sources":["email","web_fetch"]}`. The later normalized `exec` policy event and policy context both carried that accumulated provenance.
- What was not tested: Telegram Desktop integration, WhatsApp Web integration, iOS/macOS builds, Windows service install paths, and a paid/model-generated agent turn.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The live command used the PR checkout's production OpenClaw modules and active plugin registry path, but it avoided network and paid model calls by using deterministic local tool output containing production `wrapExternalContent` blocks. It proves the changed hook/provenance propagation path, not channel UI behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, wrapped tool policy hooks could run without `externalContent` after external prompt/tool content because prompt provenance was seeded into a different hook context and same-run tool-result provenance was not refreshed into the shared policy context.

## Tests and validation

Focused regression command run locally:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
  src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts \
  -t "refreshes external content provenance after a same-run external tool result" \
  --reporter=verbose
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  1 passed | 59 skipped (60)
  Duration  4.83s (transform 2.06s, setup 509ms, import 3.74s, tests 123ms, environment 0ms)
```

Regression coverage also exercises front-matter `---`, multiple production wrapper blocks, JSON-string rendered tool payloads, and forged `Source: Email` text inside untrusted content bodies.

target.title: fix: prevent Claude ACP sessions from failing on primitive adapter frames
target.body:
Closes #90404

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users spawning Claude through the ACPX runtime could get an empty ACP session when the adapter wrote a primitive JSON frame such as `1` to stdout.

## Why This Change Was Made

OpenClaw's generated ACP adapter wrappers now own a small stdout boundary before `acpx` reads adapter output. The wrapper forwards object-shaped ACP JSON-RPC messages and existing non-JSON diagnostics, but drops top-level JSON primitives, `null`, and arrays so they cannot reach the pinned ACP SDK dispatcher path that applies `"method" in message`.

The filter uses `StringDecoder` so split UTF-8 chunks are preserved, and wrapper shutdown now sets `process.exitCode` after flushing pending stdout instead of calling `process.exit()` immediately. Configured custom ACP agent commands are also launched through the generated wrapper, so custom agents such as `hermes` get the same stdout boundary as Codex and Claude.

## User Impact

Claude ACP sessions launched through OpenClaw's generated ACPX wrapper no longer fail just because the adapter emits a primitive JSON stdout frame. Valid ACP message objects and non-JSON diagnostic output keep their existing behavior.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/claude-agent-acp-completion.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.ts extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `node scripts/check-package-patches.mjs`
- `node scripts/check-dependency-pins.mjs`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`
- Standalone generated-wrapper smoke with `corepack pnpm exec tsx`: generated `claude-agent-acp-wrapper.mjs`, ran it against a local fake Claude ACP adapter that emitted `1`, `null`, `[{}]`, and a valid split-UTF-8 ACP JSON object.
- Standalone custom-Claude generated-wrapper smoke with `corepack pnpm exec tsx`: configured `agents.claude.command` to a local custom command, verified the resolved command uses `claude-agent-acp-wrapper.mjs --openclaw-run-configured`, and verified the same primitive filtering behavior.
- Standalone custom-agent generated-wrapper smoke with `corepack pnpm exec tsx`: configured `agents.hermes.command` to a local custom command, verified the resolved command uses `claude-agent-acp-wrapper.mjs --openclaw-run-configured`, and verified the same primitive filtering behavior.
- Codex sibling protocol/source check: inspected `../codex/codex-rs/app-server-transport/src/transport/stdio.rs:83` and `../codex/codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/jsonrpc_lite.rs:34`; Codex app-server stdio emits newline-delimited JSON-RPC object messages, so dropping top-level JSON primitives/arrays does not suppress valid Codex JSON-RPC frames.

Standalone smoke output:

```text
PASS standalone generated-wrapper primitive stdout smoke
forwarded: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"session/update","params":{"ok":true,"text":"雪"}}
filtered: 1, null, [{}]
PASS standalone custom-Claude generated-wrapper primitive stdout smoke
resolved command uses wrapper: true
forwarded: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"session/update","params":{"ok":true,"text":"雪"}}
filtered: 1, null, [{}]
PASS standalone custom-agent generated-wrapper primitive stdout smoke
resolved hermes uses wrapper: true
forwarded: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"session/update","params":{"ok":true,"agent":"hermes","text":"雪"}}
filtered: 1, null, [{}]
```

Real behavior proof:

Behavior addressed: generated ACPX adapter wrappers drop primitive JSON stdout frames before the ACP SDK dispatcher can see them, while valid object-shaped messages still pass through. Configured custom ACP agent commands are now launched through the generated wrapper so they get the same stdout boundary.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS using the repo ACPX Vitest config.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/acpx/src/codex-auth-bridge.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/claude-agent-acp-completion.test.ts extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts`; standalone generated-wrapper smoke with `corepack pnpm exec tsx`; standalone custom-Claude generated-wrapper smoke with `corepack pnpm exec tsx`; standalone custom-agent generated-wrapper smoke with `corepack pnpm exec tsx`.

Evidence after fix: 3 test files passed; 70 tests passed, including regression coverage for primitive/null/array stdout frames, split UTF-8 JSON text, and custom non-Claude agent wrapping. The standalone generated-wrapper smokes forwarded one valid ACP JSON object containing `雪`, filtered `1`, `null`, and `[{}]`, and confirmed custom configured Claude and `hermes` commands resolve through the generated wrapper.

Observed result after fix: the generated wrapper drops `1`, string, `null`, and array JSON lines, preserves valid ACP JSON objects, preserves multibyte JSON text split across stdout chunks, and also wraps configured custom ACP agent commands.

What was not tested: live Claude ACP `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp", agentId="claude")` smoke was not run because no Claude ACP credentials/session were available in this local validation pass.

commands.author: xydigit-zt

target.title: feat(channels): add directUserId support for per-DM model override
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- The shared `channels.modelByChannel` config surface supports channel/group/topic model overrides but cannot match ordinary direct-message (DM) conversations because the resolver never receives a stable peer identifier.

Why does this matter now?

- Operators who want different models for specific DM users (e.g., a cheaper model for high-traffic contacts) must fall back to the wildcard `"*"` entry or rely on ephemeral runtime overrides.

What is the intended outcome?

- A new optional `directUserId` field on `ChannelModelOverrideParams` lets operators pin a model to a specific DM peer. Example:
  ```json
  { "channels": { "modelByChannel": { "telegram": { "user12345": "openai/gpt-5" } } } }
  ```

- Callers derive `directUserId` from a fallback chain (`nativeDirectUserId` → `origin.from` → `origin.to` → `finalized.OriginatingTo` → `finalized.From` → `finalized.SenderId`) so all major channels (Slack, WhatsApp, Discord, Telegram, Matrix, Feishu) get DM support without per-channel code changes.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No provider-specific nested `model` fields (e.g., `channels.whatsapp.dms.*.model`).
- No extension channel code changes (Slack/WhatsApp/Discord resolvers untouched).
- No CHANGELOG.md edit.

What does success look like?

- `directUserId` added to resolver candidate keys; DM-specific config entries are matched; wildcard fallback preserved; group/thread precedence unchanged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `src/channels/model-overrides.ts` — the resolver change is the core of this PR.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #53638

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- #27014 (closed duplicate — DM model override request)
- #89532 (adjacent `/status` reporting for channel overrides)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- ClawSweeper review marked the issue as `queueable-fix`, `fix-shape-clear`, `source-repro` and confirmed the remaining gap is the DM resolver path.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** DM-specific model override missing from `channels.modelByChannel` resolver
- **Real environment tested:** local checkout built and deployed as gateway (port 19000) with live Feishu channel (WebSocket, dmPolicy=pairing)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. Applied config with modelByChannel for feishu:
   ```json
   {
     "channels": {
       "modelByChannel": {
         "feishu": {
           "feishu:ou_a8b6cab7e945387de5f253775d9b4d85": "tencent/kimi-k2.5",
           "*": "tencent/glm-5"
         }
       }
     }
   }
   ```
2. Default agent model: `tencent/glm-5`
3. Sent a Feishu DM ("你好") to the gateway while running the patched code

- **Evidence after fix:**

  The gateway log shows the DM-specific override matched — agent run used `kimi-k2.5` instead of default `glm-5`:

  ```
  2026-06-20T08:16:53.828+08:00 [feishu] Feishu[default] DM from ou_a8b6cab7e945387de5f253775d9b4d85: 你好
  2026-06-20T08:16:54.556+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run start: ... provider=tencent model=kimi-k2.5 thinking=off messageChannel=feishu
  2026-06-20T08:16:56.315+08:00 [provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=tencent api=openai-completions model=kimi-k2.5
  2026-06-20T08:17:01.768+08:00 [provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=tencent api=openai-completions model=kimi-k2.5 status=200 elapsedMs=5451
  ```

  Also confirmed with unit tests (12/12 pass):
  ```
  ✓ matches direct-user-specific model override via directUserId
  ✓ falls back to wildcard when no directUserId match exists
  ✓ matches direct-user-specific model override via directUserId from origin.from
  ✓ ignores directUserId when a groupId is present (group takes precedence)
  ✓ does not leak directUserId match into non-direct conversations
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Live Feishu DM resolved to `tencent/kimi-k2.5` (the DM-specific override) instead of the default `tencent/glm-5`. Bot replied using the overridden model.
- **Proof limitations:** Tested with Feishu channel only; all major channel callers share the same `dispatch-from-config.ts` → `resolveChannelModelOverride` path.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
pnpm test src/channels/model-overrides.test.ts
pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.config-override.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.runtime.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.inbound-context.test.ts
npx oxfmt --check src/channels/model-overrides.ts src/channels/model-overrides.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/status/status-message.ts src/agents/agent-command.ts
```

All passed.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `model-overrides.test.ts`: 5 new test cases covering DM match (telegram user ID), wildcard fallback, Slack-style `user:U12345` key from `origin.from`, group-over-DM precedence, and non-direct leakage regression.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- DMs without `groupId` could only match the wildcard `"*"` entry; specific DM peer keys in `modelByChannel` were never reached.
- A non-DM message could match a `directUserId` key when no group key matched (fixed by direct-chat gate).

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A — regression tests added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`No`)

- Existing model resolution unchanged. Only new `directUserId` paths are added; no existing paths are modified.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

- `directUserId` is an optional internal parameter, not a user-facing config key.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

- Callers passing `sessionEntry.origin?.nativeDirectUserId` may introduce a new `null`/`undefined` key in the candidate set.

How is that risk mitigated?

- `buildChannelKeyCandidates` filters null/undefined entries. Tests verify group precedence over DM keys and non-direct leakage gate. Wildcard fallback preserved.

## Current review state

### Changes in this iteration (June 20)

**P1 fix — direct-chat gate added.** `resolveChannelModelOverride` now only resolves `directUserId` candidates when `groupChatType === "direct"`, preventing DM-specific config keys from leaking into group/channel messages.

**New regression test:** `does not leak directUserId match into non-direct conversations` verifies a group message with a matching `directUserId` (but no matching group key) returns `null` instead of selecting the DM override.

All 12 tests pass (7 original + 4 DM-specific + 1 leakage regression).

**Next action:** Await ClawSweeper re-review.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): exclude durable reference pages from stale report
target.body:
## Summary

Stale Pages report flags concept and synthesis pages as "aging" even though they are intentionally durable reference material. Added page-kind exclusions so the report only evaluates freshness for source, entity, and unmanaged raw pages.

- **Problem**: The Stale Pages dashboard filter excludes `report` pages but includes `concept` and `synthesis` pages, which are intentionally durable reference material that should not be subject to staleness scoring.
- **Solution**: Add `page.kind !== "concept"` and `page.kind !== "synthesis"` to the existing filter alongside the `page.kind !== "report"` exclusion.
- **What changed**: `extensions/memory-wiki/src/compile.ts` — 3 lines added to the Stale Pages dashboard filter.
- **What did NOT change**: No config surface changes, no threshold changes, no other dashboard affected.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #93485
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

In bridge-mode vaults with many concept and synthesis pages, the Stale Pages report becomes unusable because every durable reference page is listed as "aging" regardless of content. In a real 853-source / 5-concept / 7-synthesis vault, the report's signal-to-noise ratio drops to zero because concept and synthesis pages drown out genuinely stale source pages. Users cannot work around this without lying about frontmatter (which fails lint) or patching bundled JS (which breaks on update).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: Stale Pages dashboard no longer lists concept or synthesis pages as aging/stale.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112 (x86_64), Node v22.11.0, openclaw `fix/memory-wiki-stale-pages-93485` branch (rebased on upstream/main @ 95b6df8343)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE'
import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
import path from 'node:path';
import os from 'node:os';
import { compileMemoryWikiVault } from './extensions/memory-wiki/src/compile.ts';
import { renderWikiMarkdown } from './extensions/memory-wiki/src/markdown.ts';
import { createMemoryWikiTestHarness } from './extensions/memory-wiki/src/test-helpers.ts';

const { createVault } = createMemoryWikiTestHarness();
const suiteDir = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'stale-pages-proof-'));
const { rootDir, config } = await createVault({ rootDir: path.join(suiteDir, 'vault'), initialize: true });

// Entity + Source with old updatedAt → should appear as stale
// Concept + Synthesis with old updatedAt → should be excluded
await fs.writeFile(path.join(rootDir, 'entities', 'entity-alpha.md'), renderWikiMarkdown({ frontmatter: { pageType: 'entity', id: 'entity.alpha', title: 'Alpha Entity', sourceIds: ['source.alpha'], updatedAt: '2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z' }, body: '# Alpha Entity\n' }), 'utf8');
await fs.writeFile(path.join(rootDir, 'sources', 'source-alpha.md'), renderWikiMarkdown({ frontmatter: { pageType: 'source', id: 'source.alpha', title: 'Alpha Source', updatedAt: '2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z' }, body: '# Alpha Source\n' }), 'utf8');
await fs.writeFile(path.join(rootDir, 'concepts', 'concept-beta.md'), renderWikiMarkdown({ frontmatter: { pageType: 'concept', id: 'concept.beta', title: 'Beta Concept', sourceIds: ['source.alpha'], updatedAt: '2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z' }, body: '# Beta Concept\n' }), 'utf8');
await fs.writeFile(path.join(rootDir, 'syntheses', 'synthesis-gamma.md'), renderWikiMarkdown({ frontmatter: { pageType: 'synthesis', id: 'synthesis.gamma', title: 'Gamma Synthesis', sourceIds: ['source.alpha'], updatedAt: '2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z' }, body: '# Gamma Synthesis\n' }), 'utf8');

const result = await compileMemoryWikiVault(config);
const stalePages = await fs.readFile(path.join(rootDir, 'reports', 'stale-pages.md'), 'utf8');
console.log(stalePages);
console.log('Entity in stale-pages:', stalePages.includes('Alpha Entity'));
console.log('Source in stale-pages:', stalePages.includes('Alpha Source'));
console.log('Concept in stale-pages:', stalePages.includes('Beta Concept'));
console.log('Synthesis in stale-pages:', stalePages.includes('Gamma Synthesis'));
await fs.rm(suiteDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
NODE
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
---
pageType: report
id: report.stale-pages
title: Stale Pages
status: active
updatedAt: 2026-06-18T07:03:38.580Z
---

# Stale Pages

## Generated
<!-- openclaw:wiki:stale-pages:start -->
- Stale pages: 2

- [Alpha Entity](../entities/entity-alpha.md): stale (2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z)
- [Alpha Source](../sources/source-alpha.md): stale (2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z)
<!-- openclaw:wiki:stale-pages:end -->

=== Verification ===
Entity in stale-pages:   YES ✓
Source in stale-pages:    YES ✓
Concept in stale-pages:   EXCLUDED ✓
Synthesis in stale-pages: EXCLUDED ✓
```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Concept and synthesis pages with old updatedAt are excluded from stale-pages report.
  2. Entity and source pages with the same old updatedAt still appear as stale.
  3. The `report` exclusion (pre-existing) is unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: Browser UI flow; multi-vault compile with conflicting source paths.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The Stale Pages dashboard filter at `extensions/memory-wiki/src/compile.ts:216` only excluded `page.kind !== "report"`, with no awareness that concept and synthesis pages are intentionally durable and should not be scored for staleness.

**Provenance**: The stale-pages filter was implemented with the initial memory-wiki compile dashboard set — all dashboard pages use the same filter pattern. No single commit introduced this as a regression; it is the original design.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- **Target file**: `extensions/memory-wiki/src/compile.test.ts` — new `excludes concept and synthesis pages from stale-pages report` test
- **Coverage**: Creates a temporary vault with entity, source, concept, and synthesis pages (all with old updatedAt), runs `compileMemoryWikiVault`, then asserts entity/source are present and concept/synthesis are absent from `reports/stale-pages.md`
- **Minimum protection rationale**: 1 vault × 4 page kinds = direct coverage of the filter boundary without test infrastructure overhead

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Stale Pages report will no longer list concept or synthesis pages as aging/stale. No other user-visible changes.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: Source code diff review; filter logic traces through all branches; real vault compile output confirmed exclusion behavior.
- **Edge cases checked**: Concept and synthesis page kinds confirmed in `WIKI_PAGE_KINDS` array; both kinds exist in the compile's page group definitions; other dashboards (Person Agent Directory) use a separate `isPersonLikePage` filter that is unaffected; regression test covers the 4-page-kind boundary.
- **What you did NOT verify**: Browser UI flow; multi-vault compile with conflicting source paths.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The minimal change matches the problem scope exactly: exclude the two known-durable page kinds from the stale-pages freshness check. A configurable solution (as proposed in the issue) would add unnecessary config surface per OpenClaw policy — a better default is the right approach.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The filter is isolated to one dashboard definition within the `DASHBOARD_PAGES` array.
- **Alternative considered**: Configurable `excludeKinds` in memory-wiki config (rejected per ClawSweeper review: OpenClaw policy sets a high bar for new config knobs; a better default is preferred).

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Full open-source-pr skill workflow; issue analysis, code review, provenance tracing, and implementation.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: Users who intentionally want concept/synthesis staleness tracked can no longer see them in the report.
- **Mitigation**: Concept and synthesis pages are by design durable reference material (per memory-wiki documentation); they are expected to remain untouched for months. Any user who needs staleness tracking can re-categorize content as source or entity pages.
- **Compatibility**: All existing report behavior for source, entity, and unmanaged raw source pages is unchanged.

---

Related to #93485

commands.author: Ittiz

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: Open, Download, and Copy.
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably.
- Retries managed-image preview/action fetches with ordered Control UI auth candidates after `401/403`, so stale read-token credentials do not block owner-capable credentials.
- Binds requester-session headers to authenticated owner/admin context before serving managed image bytes.
- Renders generated image frames with stable rounded clipping, known-size geometry, and reserved pending-preview space.
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract.
- Restacked on current `main` and the clean #75727 head; the previous stale media/trust history is no longer in this PR head.
- Removed the public `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` config surface; Control UI thumbnails use the internal default.

## Why

Generated images were displayed without the full media affordances expected in chat. The implementation needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry, full-size action targets, and a safer managed-image auth path that does not strand a browser on stale credentials.

## Real behavior proof

- Current head tested: `5fbacf09ca6a7b5bfea79c8f0970519603b874ff`.
- Behavior addressed: generated/managed chat images render as authenticated thumbnail previews, Open/Download/Copy use the full image, owner-capable credentials are retried after stale `401/403` credentials, and the public thumbnail-size config knob is gone.
- Real environment tested: local Linux checkout after restacking on current `main`, Vitest gateway/UI/config shards, and oxlint.

Commands run after the current fix:

```bash
git diff --check
rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Evidence:

```text
$ rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
# no matches

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 127.90s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 304.41s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` now verifies managed-image thumbnail and full-image action fetches try `Bearer session-token`, receive `403`, then retry and succeed with `Bearer owner-token` while preserving `x-openclaw-requester-session-key`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` verifies ordered unique auth candidates.
- `src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` now verifies bounded thumbnail generation without exposing a public config key.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No new user permission; generated image UI actions use existing media routes.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. The browser now retries existing ordered Control UI auth candidates for same-origin managed image routes after `401/403`.
- Data access scope changed? The server still requires owner/admin context for managed image bytes; the UI retry path only lets an already-present owner-capable credential recover from a stale first credential.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No. The proposed public thumbnail-size config was removed before merge.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: fix(matrix): recognize MiniMax mm: namespaced reasoning tags in monitor suppression
target.body:
## Summary

#93767 brought MiniMax `mm:` namespace support to reasoning-tag handling and noted the same `antml:`-only / no-namespace pattern is duplicated across monitor paths. Those have since been covered — src paths (#93806, merged), Slack monitor preview (#93874, merged), iMessage monitor (#93820) — leaving the **Matrix monitor's hand-rolled suppression fallback** as the last gap:

- `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.ts` — `THINKING_TAG_RE` / `THINKING_BLOCK_RE`, used by `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` (the content-based fallback when a reply isn't already flagged `isReasoning`). Both regexes were still `(?:think(?:ing)?|thought|antthinking)` — no `antml:`, no `mm:`.

Result: a reply containing only `<mm:think>…</mm:think>` that wasn't pre-flagged `isReasoning` is not recognized as reasoning and gets delivered verbatim into the Matrix room, leaking the internal chain-of-thought.

## Changes

- Both `THINKING_TAG_RE` and `THINKING_BLOCK_RE`: `(?:think(?:ing)?|thought|antthinking)` → `(?:(?:antml:|mm:)?(?:think(?:ing)?|thought)|antthinking)`, matching the shared contract in `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`. The change is strictly confined to the two regex constants — `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` and the delivery semantics are untouched (orthogonal to in-flight PRs #93696/#93830 which alter delivery routing).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: a Matrix reply whose content is only a MiniMax `mm:`-namespaced reasoning tag (`<mm:think>…</mm:think>`) and is not pre-flagged `isReasoning` must be recognized as reasoning by the monitor fallback and suppressed (not delivered into the room), instead of being sent verbatim and leaking the chain-of-thought. A plain visible reply must still be delivered.

**Real environment tested**: Node v22.22.0 + tsx, driving the real production function `deliverMatrixReplies` imported directly from the patched `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.ts` (which calls the changed `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` / `THINKING_TAG_RE` / `THINKING_BLOCK_RE`). The only mocked boundary is the outermost Matrix transport `../send.js` (a stub recording what is actually handed to `sendMessageMatrix`), redirected via a node 22 `module.register()` loader hook; the runtime is injected through the real `setMatrixRuntime`. Read-back boundary = the messages the transport actually receives.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/replies.test.ts --run`
- standalone real-runtime harness `proof.mts`: `register(loader)` → dynamic-import patched `replies.ts` → `setMatrixRuntime(stub)` → `deliverMatrixReplies({ replies: [{ text: "<mm:think>secret-reasoning-should-be-hidden</mm:think>" }, { text: "the real visible answer" }], roomId: "room:proof", replyToMode: "off", … })` → assert what reached the transport stub.
- Negative control: restore the parent-commit `replies.ts` (revert the regex change) and re-run the same harness.

**Evidence after fix**:
- Vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 6 passed (6)`.
- Harness (patched):
  ```
  messages actually delivered to transport: ["the real visible answer"]
  mm: reasoning secret LEAKED to transport : false
  plain visible answer delivered           : true
  RESULT: PASS (patched) — mm: reasoning suppressed (not delivered), visible answer delivered.
  ```
- Harness (negative control, fix reverted):
  ```
  messages actually delivered to transport: ["<mm:think>secret-reasoning-should-be-hidden</mm:think>","the real visible answer"]
  mm: reasoning secret LEAKED to transport : true
  RESULT: BUG (pre-patch / reverted) — mm: reasoning tag NOT recognized -> secret leaked to transport.
  ```

**Observed result after fix**: with the patch, the `<mm:think>…</mm:think>` reply is suppressed by `shouldSuppressReasoningReplyText` and never handed to `sendMessageMatrix` (transport receives only `the real visible answer`); reverting the two regex constants makes the same reply leak verbatim into the transport. This confirms the regex change is what closes the leak, and that ordinary visible text is still delivered (no over-suppression).

**What was not tested**: a live Matrix homeserver / federation round-trip and the real `sendMessageMatrix` network path were stubbed at the transport boundary; this proof exercises the suppression decision and the exact content handed to the transport, not Matrix server delivery, encryption, or chunking on a real room.

## Scope / context

Final follow-up in the #93767 `mm:` series (src #93806, Slack #93874 merged; iMessage #93820 open). Matrix and iMessage were the two hand-rolled fallback gaps; iMessage is #93820, this closes Matrix. Orthogonal to #93696/#93830 (delivery routing) — confined to the regex constants.

target.title: fix(usage): reject inverted startDate-endDate range in usage.cost and sessions.usage
target.body:
## Summary

#93745 added `findInvalidExplicitDate` to reject invalid calendar dates in `usage.cost` / `sessions.usage` (merged). But `parseDateRange` in `src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts` does not validate `startMs <= endMs` when both dates are explicitly given — an inverted range (startDate after endDate) yields a backwards window, and the cost loader silently returns empty while `respond(true)` succeeds. This is the symmetric gap to #93745 (same "silently wrong window" family; sibling to merged #93903's cron absolute-timestamp validation).

## Changes

- Added `hasInvertedExplicitDateRange` helper (reusing extracted `hasExplicitDateValue`). Both `usage.cost` and `sessions.usage` now reject `startDate > endDate` with `INVALID_REQUEST: "startDate must not be after endDate"` before touching runtime config / cost loader / session scan. `parseDateRange`'s return contract is unchanged (validation is at the handler entry). `testApi` exposes the helper. +96/-1 across 2 files (one is the test).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: an inverted explicit date range (`startDate` after `endDate`) used to be silently accepted by `usage.cost` / `sessions.usage` — `parseDateRange` produced a backwards window (`startMs > endMs`), the real cost/session loader was reached with that backwards window, and `respond(true, ...)` returned an empty/wrong result with no error. The fix rejects it with `respond(false, INVALID_REQUEST "startDate must not be after endDate")` before any loader is touched.

**Real environment tested**: real exported `usageHandlers["usage.cost"]` and `usageHandlers["sessions.usage"]` imported from the changed `src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts` and driven under tsx on Node v22.22.0 (Linux). Only the outermost data IO in `src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts` (`loadCostUsageSummaryFromCache`, `discoverAllSessions`, `loadSessionLogs`) was replaced — via a `module.register()` loader hook — with recorders that capture the exact `(startMs,endMs)` window each handler computes and then return empty data. Everything else in the handler (param validation, `hasInvertedExplicitDateRange`, `resolveDateInterpretation`, `parseDateRange`) runs as production code.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. `git worktree add` PR head `fix/usage-reject-inverted-date-range`.
2. tsx real-runtime proof drives both handlers with an inverted range `{startDate: "2026-02-03", endDate: "2026-02-02"}` and reads back the `respond(ok, payload, error)` args plus whether the real loader was reached and with what window.
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts --run`.
4. Negative control: restore the pre-PR (parent) `usage.ts` (fix removed, `hasInvertedExplicitDateRange` absent) and re-run the same proof.
5. Forward-range control `{startDate: "2026-02-01", endDate: "2026-02-02"}` driven through both handlers to confirm the fix is not over-broad.

**Evidence after fix** (patched tree):
```
[sanity] inverted range parseDateToMs: startMs=1770076800000 endMs=1769990400000 startMs>endMs=true
---- usage.cost : INVERTED range {start=2026-02-03, end=2026-02-02} ----
  respond.ok      = false
  respond.error   = {"code":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"startDate must not be after endDate"}
  reached loader? = false
  => PATCHED behavior: REJECTED (respond.false + INVALID_REQUEST, loader NOT reached)
---- sessions.usage : INVERTED range {start=2026-02-03, end=2026-02-02} ----
  respond.ok      = false
  respond.error   = {"code":"INVALID_REQUEST","message":"startDate must not be after endDate"}
  reached loader? = false
  => PATCHED behavior: REJECTED
---- negative control: FORWARD range {start=2026-02-01, end=2026-02-02} must still pass ----
  usage.cost:     respond.ok=true reachedLoader=true err=null
  sessions.usage: respond.ok=true reachedLoader=true err=null
RESULT: PASS
```
Negative control (parent `usage.ts`, fix removed) — inverted range is silently accepted, the real loader is reached with a **backwards** window, no error:
```
fix present in this checkout? hasInvertedExplicitDateRange = undefined
---- usage.cost : INVERTED range {start=2026-02-03, end=2026-02-02} ----
  respond.ok      = true
  respond.error   = null
  reached loader? = true  loadCostUsageSummaryFromCache(startMs=1770076800000,endMs=1770076799999,backwards=true)
  => PRE-PATCH BUG: SILENTLY ACCEPTED inverted range
---- sessions.usage : INVERTED range {start=2026-02-03, end=2026-02-02} ----
  respond.ok      = true
  respond.error   = null
  reached loader? = true  discoverAllSessions(startMs=1770076800000,endMs=1770076799999,backwards=true)
  => PRE-PATCH BUG: SILENTLY ACCEPTED inverted range
```
Vitest: `2 files / 44 tests passed` (28s), including the new helper boundary case and the table-driven `usage.cost` / `sessions.usage` rejection case.

**Observed result after fix**: both handlers reject the inverted range with `respond(false, undefined, {code: "INVALID_REQUEST", message: "startDate must not be after endDate"})` and the cost/session loader is never invoked; the forward range `{2026-02-01 → 2026-02-02}` still resolves and reaches the loader (`respond(true)`), so the fix only rejects genuinely inverted ranges. Reverting the source flips this back to silent acceptance with a backwards `startMs > endMs` window.

**What was not tested**: real on-disk cost/session data aggregation (the loaders were stubbed to return empty data — they are the layer below the validation under test); the rejection happens before any loader runs, so loader internals are out of scope here. UI/client surfacing of the new error and timezone/`utcOffset` interaction beyond the parsed-window comparison were not exercised.

## Scope / context

No linked issue — symmetric follow-up to merged #93745 (usage invalid-date rejection) and #93903 (cron invalid absolute timestamp). No competing PR touches this gap.
